overview.short: Kurdugad Fort is a fort located 143 km (89 mi) from Mumbai and 113km from Pune in Raigad district, of Maharashtra.
overview.description: Kurdugad Fort is a fort located 143 km (89 mi) from Mumbai and 113km from Pune in Raigad district, of Maharashtra. This fort was an important fort in Raigad district as a watch over for the trade route from Pune to coastal ports. The fort is surrounded by thick forest and hill slopes.

topPlaces.name: Baneshwar
topPlaces.description: Baneshwar Temple The word "Baneshwar" is coined with a very literal sense where "Ban" means Forest and Ishwar stands for God. So when the God resides within a forest, the place can be rightly named as Baneshwar.

topPlaces.name: Mangad Fort
topPlaces.description: Mangad Fort is a survey fort which is situated in Borwadi, an area near Mashidwadi in Mangaon, a taluka in Raigad district in the Indian state of Maharashtra. The fort is made sacred by the footsteps of the founder of Hindavi Swaraj, the famous Maratha king Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaja.

topPlaces.name: Tamhini Ghat
topPlaces.description: Tamhini Ghat is a mountain passage located between Mulshi and Tamhini in Maharashtra, India. Situated on the crest of the Western Ghat mountain ranges, Tamhini Ghat is noted for its surroundings comprising scenic waterfalls, lakes and dense woods.

items.note: Baneshwar Temple The word "Baneshwar" is coined with a very literal sense where "Ban" means Forest and Ishwar stands for God. So when the God resides within a forest, the place can be rightly named as Baneshwar.

Mangad Fort is a survey fort which is situated in Borwadi, an area near Mashidwadi in Mangaon, a taluka in Raigad district in the Indian state of Maharashtra. The fort is made sacred by the footsteps of the founder of Hindavi Swaraj, the famous Maratha king Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaja.

Tamhini Ghat is a mountain passage located between Mulshi and Tamhini in Maharashtra, India. Situated on the crest of the Western Ghat mountain ranges, Tamhini Ghat is noted for its surroundings comprising scenic waterfalls, lakes and dense woods.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kurdugad?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kurdugad is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

The nearest airport is Pune Airport (59 km) and the nearest railway station is Pune Junction (53 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kurdugad?
faq.a: 2 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kurdugad at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kurdugad?
faq.a: Stays in Kurdugad start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kurdugad?
faq.a: The top attractions are Baneshwar, Mangad Fort, Tamhini Ghat.
